holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is up 13% over five days on the Apple foundry partnership headline, stacking on prior Nvidia/Tesla domestic manufacturing news — the position is working and the narrative is fresh, but there's no dated company catalyst on the board to anchor an add or trim. Triple witching tomorrow can amplify the move in either direction given how stretched the tape is, but it's a flow event, not a fundamental one. Without a confirmed earnings date or follow-on Apple deal specifics, let the winner run and wait for the next dated catalyst to define the window.
INTC.bull: Apple foundry win validates Intel's US manufacturing thesis and opens the door to more hyperscaler/OEM deal flow.
INTC.bear: A 13% five-day run with no earnings date to backstop it is vulnerable to a triple-witching reversal or headline fade.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NVDA — just triple witching tomorrow, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. Position is small (8 shares), modestly green on the week and only -1.3% today, so there's nothing forcing action; the -1.3% into op-ex is more likely dealer/gamma noise than a signal. Wait for the next earnings date to confirm before leaning either way; until then, treat tomorrow's tape as noise.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro flow event; no company catalyst dated)
NVDA.bull: AI capex cycle intact and position is small enough that op-ex chop doesn't matter — let the next print be the catalyst.
NVDA.bear: Without a dated company catalyst, NVDA trades on macro/positioning; a heavy triple witching unwind could extend today's weakness.

ORCL.read: ORCL is -9% over five days into triple witching tomorrow, with no company-specific catalyst on the calendar and below-average volume (0.64 rel vol) suggesting positioning/derisking rather than fundamental news. Without a dated Oracle event to anchor to, the next 24 hours are about whether the witching-day flows mark a washout low or extend the slide; the actual add window is more likely defined by the next earnings print (typically mid-June for FY Q4, but not confirmed here) than by macro mechanics.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (no ORCL-specific catalyst on calendar)
ORCL.bull: Drawdown is on weak volume into a known liquidity event — a witching-day flush followed by stabilization would set up a reclaim of the 5-day breakdown.
ORCL.bear: No Oracle-specific catalyst means no near-term fundamental reason for the slide to stop; if witching brings forced selling and the tape stays heavy, the -9% leg can extend before any earnings cushion arrives.

NOK.read: No dated company catalysts on the board for NOK right now — just triple witching tomorrow, which can add tape noise but isn't a fundamental driver for this name. The position is small (~$2.4k), up modestly on the week and only off 1.2% today, so there's nothing in the price action forcing a decision. Without a confirmed earnings date or capital-return event in view, this is a wait-for-the-next-catalyst situation rather than an add/trim window.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro tape event, not NOK-specific)
NOK.bull: 5-day is green and the pullback today is shallow; AI/datacenter networking narrative keeps a bid under the name.
NOK.bear: No dated company catalyst means no clear edge, and a witching-day flush could drag low-beta telco names with the broader tape.

NBIS.read: NBIS is +32.8% over five sessions and +5.9% today with no dated company catalyst on the board — that's a momentum tape running into tomorrow's triple witching, where index/options unwinds can amplify moves in high-beta AI names like this one. With no earnings or guidance event to anchor the move, the asymmetry into a known liquidity event favors scaling some risk; you can always re-engage on a flush. Without a confirmed next print, this is a tape-driven read, not a fundamental one.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro liquidity event, not company-specific)
NBIS.bull: AI/GPU-cloud momentum is intact and a vol-driven shakeout would just refresh the setup.
NBIS.bear: A 33% five-day rip into triple witching with no catalyst is exactly the kind of move that gives back fast.

KO.read: KO has no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the feed — and the stock is soft (-3.7% on the week, -0.5% today) on what looks like macro positioning ahead of tomorrow's triple witching. Without a binary print to trade around, there's no real add/trim window here; the weakness is tape, not thesis. Wait for an earnings date or ex-div to firm up before leaning either way.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not KO-specific)
KO.bull: Defensive staple selling off on options-expiry flow, not fundamentals — pullback into a quality name.
KO.bear: No dated catalyst to mark a turn; drift can continue if rates/FX stay against staples.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a fresh June 2026 IPO with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — there's no dated company catalyst to anchor an add/trim window against. The nearest scheduled event is tomorrow's triple witching (2026-06-19), which can amplify volatility in a newly-listed, thinly-distributed float but isn't a fundamental catalyst. Sit tight until the first earnings date is calendar-confirmed or lockup/coverage initiation dates surface.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro only; no company-specific date yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity-value IPO with unique exposure; first earnings print as a public company could re-rate the multiple if Starlink ARR is disclosed cleanly.
SPCX.bear: Post-IPO float dynamics, lockup overhang, and tomorrow's triple witching can drive outsized swings with no fundamental anchor to defend the price.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
